output.summary: Removed the erroneous editorScript field from CardsGridItem/block.json. The child block's TypeScript is already compiled into the parent bundle via webpack's glob entry pattern, so the editorScript declaration was causing WordPress to attempt loading the raw .tsx file directly in the browser, resulting in a SyntaxError.

filesModified.purpose: Removed editorScript field to match pattern used by all other child blocks in the theme

warnings: Asset size limit warnings (pre-existing, not related to this fix)
Webpack performance recommendations (pre-existing, not related to this fix)

syncStatus.notes: This fix only affects block registration metadata in block.json. No TSX/PHP sync changes were needed.

testPage.notes: No new test page needed. This is a fix to an existing block. QA should verify using existing CardsGrid test pages.

fixesApplied.issue: Browser console error: 'Uncaught SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module' when CardsGrid block is used
fixesApplied.rationale: The editorScript field was causing WordPress to enqueue the raw TypeScript source file, which browsers cannot parse. The webpack config already compiles all block index files via glob pattern (glob.sync('./blocks/**/index.{js,jsx,ts,tsx}')) into the bundled editor.js output. All 11 other child blocks in the theme (AccordionItem, CardsCarouselItem, GalleryCarouselItem, TabsItem, Button, TextCard, ImageCard, QuickLinkItem, FooterInfo, FooterNav, FooterCredits, HeaderMain) omit this field and work correctly.

nextSteps: QA should verify: (1) No browser console errors on pages with CardsGrid blocks, (2) CardsGrid and CardsGridItem render correctly in the block editor, (3) CardsGridItem can be added/edited/removed within CardsGrid, (4) Frontend rendering is unaffected
